events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: worker clipped lanyard to top rail of temporary guardrail, guardrail built to standard guardrail strength not personal fall arrest anchorage ratings. Task: judge whether tie-off acceptable for five-minute task. Address difference between guardrail strength and anchorage requirements.

We need decision fail because non-compliant. Risk high. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 50? Value 0. Sound_source etc default empty string/0. Findings: tie-off to guardrail top rail not rated as PFAS anchor; guardrail strength requirements (200 lb top rail, etc) differ from anchorage requirements (5,000 lb per worker or designed by qualified person with safety factor); task duration irrelevant; nearest engineered anchor 40 ft away doesn't justify improper anchor; worker exposed to fall from roof edge. Actions: immediate stop work, remove lanyard from guardrail, provide/reposition compliant anchor or use alternative fall protection (e.g., mobile anchor, boom lift, warning line + monitor? For construction over 6 ft need guardrail/safety net/PFAS). Ensure worker tied off to approved anchor; retrain; inspect equipment. Rationale. References: OSHA 1926.502(d)(15) anchorage capable of supporting 5,000 lb per worker or designed by qualified person; 1926.502(b)(3) guardrail top rail strength 200 lb; 1926.501(b)(10) roof work fall protection; 1926.502(d)(16) personal fall arrest systems. Need JSON valid. Ensure no prose outside JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Worker tied off to the top rail of a temporary guardrail that is not rated or installed as a personal fall arrest anchor.",
    "Guardrails are designed to resist 200 lbf applied to the top rail, not the 5,000 lbf anchorage load required for a personal fall arrest system.",
    "A five-minute exposure does not reduce the fall hazard or permit use of an inadequate anchor.",
    "The nearest compliant anchor being 40 ft away does not justify improvising an unapproved tie-off point."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the work immediately and have the worker disconnect from the guardrail.",
    "Provide a code-compliant anchorage (engineered anchor, structural member, or manufacturer-rated temporary anchor) within reach of the work area.",
    "If a compliant anchor cannot be placed, use an alternative fall-protection system such as a mobile anchor, aerial lift, or reposition the work to a protected location.",
    "Inspect the lanyard and harness for damage from the improper tie-off.",
    "Retrain the worker and crew on approved anchor selection and the difference between guardrail strength and fall-arrest anchorage requirements."
  ],
  "rationale": "A temporary guardrail top rail meets guardrail strength requirements, not personal fall arrest anchorage requirements. OSHA requires PFAS anchorages to support 5,000 lb per worker or be designed by a qualified person with adequate safety factor. Tying off to an unrated guardrail creates a high fall risk and is non-compliant regardless of task duration.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.502(d)(15) - Anchorage strength requirements for personal fall arrest systems",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.502(b)(3) - Guardrail top rail strength requirements",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.501(b)(10) - Fall protection for roofing work",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.502(d)(16) - Personal fall arrest system criteria"
  ]
}
